classObjective: Identify exponential behavior from data using common ratios.
workedExamples: 8-1, Example 1 — Identify Exponential Behavior from Data
warmUp: Calculate the ratio between consecutive terms: 2, 6, 18, 54
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: common ratio test; recognizing growth vs. decay from tables; distinguishing exponential from linear patterns
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice identifying exponential behavior in data sets using common ratio calculation
independentPractice: Practice problems identifying exponential behavior from tables and data sets
assessment: Exit ticket: determine whether a given data set is exponential, and if so, find the common ratio
capReflection: How does the common ratio test distinguish exponential functions from linear functions?
